channel.title: Entertainment News | CableTV.com
channel.description: Super fans, rejoice! Our entertainment news keeps you passionate about sports, TV, and streaming.

item.title: DIRECTV Streaming vs. DIRECTV Satellite: Live Streaming or Satellite TV Service – Which Is Better For You?
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: DIRECTV’s streaming and satellite products both have some excellent features, but they’re both way too expensive to be worth it for most folks. Forced to choose one over the other, we’d pick DIRECTV’s streaming plans since they’re the most flexible (no contract means an easier escape) and avoid DIRECTV’s ridiculous second-year price hike.
item.content:encoded:
Most Streaming Sports Channels
DIRECTV Streaming
Editorial rating (3.60/5)
$89.99–$169.99/mo.
No contracts
Fast self-installation; no device requirement
Many sports channels, regional sports networks, and add-ons
5-day free trial
High pricing for a streaming TV service
Equipment fees
View Plans for DIRECTV STREAM
Compare top features
$89.99–$169.99/mo.
No contracts
Fast self-installation; no device requirement
Many sports channels, regional sports networks, and add-ons
5-day free trial
High pricing for a streaming TV service
Equipment fees
Best TV Provider for Sports
DIRECTV Satellite
Editorial rating (4.30/5)
$89.99–$169.99/mo.
2-yr. price guarantee
Genie DVR or wireless Gemini devices
Many sports channels, regional sports networks, and add-ons
2-yr. contract
No self-installation
High prices
View Plans for DIRECTV
Compare top features
$89.99–$169.99/mo.
2-yr. price guarantee
Genie DVR or wireless Gemini devices
Many sports channels, regional sports networks, and add-ons
2-yr. contract
No self-installation
High prices

Data effective as of post date. Not all offers are available in all areas. See full disclaimer for details.

Status: Verified on 4/7/26

Is DIRECTV streaming or DIRECTV satellite better?

When it comes to DIRECTV, I have years of experience using both the classic satellite dish setup and its streaming form. Which one is better depends on your preferences. Each DIRECTV service has identical high prices, two-year term agreements, and hidden fees that always seem to pop up. Also, both services are excellent for sports. The biggest differences are channel lineups, delivery method (satellite vs. streaming), devices (Genie or Gemini or Gemini Air) for live TV channels, movies, and shows. I’ve enjoyed both feature-rich services, but if you forced me to choose, I’d go with DIRECTV’s streaming plans. It’s just simpler to install and use—and cancel without a penalty. However, my overall recommendation is that DIRECTV plans change too frequently and its prices are much too high; you can often get more bang for your buck elsewhere. Check out YouTube TV or Hulu + Live TV or, if you’re still considering a satellite setup, DISH. But maybe you’re not yet ready to turn your back on DIRECTV—perhaps because you want to access the services’ top-shelf sportsball experience. If so, read on for a deeper comparison of both services.

What else is out there?

You can get streaming TV services wherever you can get a steady internet connection. Satellite TV services like DISH and DIRECTV are available almost anywhere. But cable TV providers’ availability depends on your location. Enter your zip code below to find providers in your area.

Compare DIRECTV streaming and satellite plans head-to-head

Provider Price* Connection type Channel count DVR storage Simultaneous recordings Details
DIRECTV (streaming)
Best for flexibility 		$89.99–$169.99/mo. for 24 mos. plus taxes and fees Internet 90–185+ Unlimited for $10.00/mo. 5 View Plans for DIRECTV STREAM

Read DIRECTV Streaming Review
DIRECTV (satellite)
Best for DVR and features 		$89.99–$169.99/mo. for 24 mos. plus taxes and fees Satellite 165–340+ 200 hrs. (Genie) or 450 hrs. (Gemini) for $10.00/mo. 3 View Plans for DIRECTV

Read DIRECTV Satellite Review

Data effective as of post date. Not all offers are available in all areas. See full disclaimer for details.

Let’s recap the similarities and differences between DIRECTV’s streaming and satellite plans. DIRECTV’s streaming plans have 90–185+ channels—that’s a good bit less than its satellite plans. That’s not such a big deal because the lineups are curated to include mostly popular channels—you won’t get as many filler channels as you find on DIRECTV. DIRECTV’s streaming plans connect to your home Wi-Fi network and deliver your channels via either the Gemini Air device (first one’s free), or one of several streaming devices that support the DIRECTV app.Pro tip: If DIRECTV’s traditional plans are too expensive, but you like the brand, check out DIRECTV’s Genre Packs. These themed packages are dedicated to entertainment, kids, news, and sports content, featuring smaller price tags. They also have fewer channels—but more of the ones you want—and some even include streaming services like Disney+, ESPN Select, Hulu, and HBO Max.

If satellite TV is your only option, we think DIRECTV is the best in the biz. Still, some folks love DISH Network, which has slightly fewer channels than DIRECTV, but almost as many sports networks and add-ons. DISH also has the more powerful Hopper 3 DVR and similarly guarantees your monthly payment for two years. However, as I mentioned, I steer people away from DIRECTV and towards live TV streaming services like YouTube TV or Hulu + Live TV. While they have fewer channels than DIRECTV, I’ve found they offer better overall value with better affordability, no hidden fees, curated lineups of popular channels, and free unlimited cloud DVR storage.

DIRECTV streaming vs. satellite prices and plans: Both are expensive

DIRECTV’s streaming and satellite services each have four plans with similar monthly payments—but wildly divergent channel counts. Let’s see how they rate in terms of overall value.

DIRECTV streaming pricing and plans

Package Price* Channel count Popular channels Details
ENTERTAINMENT $89.99/mo. for 24 mos. plus taxes and fees 90+ AMC, CNN, ESPN, Hallmark Channel, HGTV, MSNBC, Nickelodeon, and TLC View Plan
CHOICE™ $94.99/mo. for 24 mos. plus taxes and fees 125+ Bravo, Disney, IFC, Nick Jr., OWN, Science, Travel Channel, and UniMás View Plan
ULTIMATE $124.99/mo. for 24 mos. plus taxes and fees 160+ Magnolia Network, Golf Channel, LOGO, Nat Geo WILD, NHL Network, and Universal Kids View Plan
PREMIER $169.99/mo. for 24 mos. plus taxes and fees 185+ CINEMAX®, STARZ®, Paramount+ Premium and HBO MaxTM View Plan

Data effective as of post date. Not all offers are available in all areas. See full disclaimer for details.

All live TV streaming services, not just DIRECTV, have fewer channels than cable and satellite TV services. So it’s not like DIRECTV is trying to give you less for your money. It’s how live TV streaming services roll—they offer quality over quantity or more popular channels instead of filler networks. The streaming CHOICE plan ($94.99/mo., 125+ channels) is the best value. With CHOICE and ULTIMATE ($124.99/mo., 160 channels) packages, new customers get three free months of CINEMAX, MGM+, HBO Max, Paramount+ Premium, and STARZ. But if you go with PREMIER ($169.99/mo., 185+ channels), those premium channels are a permanent part of your plan.

See All DIRECTV Streaming Channels

DIRECTV satellite pricing and plans

Package Price* Channel count Popular channels Details
ENTERTAINMENT $89.99/mo. for 24 mos. plus taxes and fees 175+ AMC, CNN, ESPN, Hallmark Channel, HGTV, MSNBC, Nickelodeon, and TLC View Plan
CHOICE™ $94.99/mo. for 24 mos. plus taxes and fees 215+ Bravo, Disney, IFC, Nick Jr., OWN, Science, Travel Channel, and UniMás View Plan
ULTIMATE $124.99/mo. for 24 mos. plus taxes and fees 270+ Magnolia Network, Golf Channel, LOGO, Nat Geo WILD, NHL Network, and Universal Kids View Plan
PREMIER™ $169.99/mo. for 24 mos. plus taxes and fees 350+ CINEMAX, STARZ, Paramount+ Premium, and HBO Max View Plan

Data effective as of post date. Not all offers are available in all areas. See full disclaimer for details.

DIRECTV’s satellite and streaming packages are essentially the same in terms of popular channels. The difference is that the streaming packages cut out the filler channels you’ll probably never watch. Both providers now charge close to the same price for their plans—but the channel counts are vastly different, as I’ve shown.

See All DIRECTV Satellite Channels
Pro tip: Check out our guide to the Hidden Costs of DIRECTV, which explains all of the service’s various hidden charges, including fees for activation, devices, early cancellation, late payments, local channels, and more.

DIRECTV streaming and satellite plans have different equipment and DVR storage

When it comes to DVRs, the DIRECTV Genie (200 hours) and Genie 2 (450 hours) are powerful units with limited streaming app support. That said, the DIRECTV Gemini (450–unlimited hours) and Gemini Air (unlimited hours) aren’t as feature-rich as the Genies, but their accompanying cloud DVR storage is much greater. Check out each device’s vital statistics below.

DIRECTV streaming vs. satellite DVR

DVR Price Storage Simultaneous recordings Video quality Streaming app support Details
DIRECTV Gemini Air (optional) $10.00/mo. Unlimited (cloud) 3 Up to 4K Disney+, ESPN, Hulu, HBO Max, Paramount+, Peacock View Plans for DIRECTV
DIRECTV Genie $10.00/mo. 200 hrs. (physical) 5 Up to 4K HBO Max, Netflix, Pandora View Plans for DIRECTV
DIRECTV Genie 2 (wireless) $10.00/mo. 450 hrs. (cloud) 7 Up to 4K HBO Max, Netflix, Pandora View Plans for DIRECTV
DIRECTV Gemini $10.00/mo. 450 hrs.–unlimited (cloud) 3 Up to 4K Disney+, ESPN, Hulu, HBO Max, Netflix, Paramount+, Peacock, Prime Video View Plans for DIRECTV

Data effective as of post date. Not all offers are available in all areas. See full disclaimer for details.

DIRECTV Gemini Air and cloud DVR

When you sign up for DIRECTV’s streaming plans, you can choose to use the service’s new Gemini Air device ($10.00 per month), which includes a universal voice remote, support for most major streaming apps, and unlimited DVR. Or you can save ten bucks a month by using one of the many streaming devices, smartphones, or tablets that you probably already own—and already support the DIRECTV app. Unlimited cloud DVR has a nine-month keep time for recordings, and that sure beats DIRECTV’s old cloud DVR offer: A measly 20 hours free or unlimited storage for $10.00 a month, with only a 90-day keep time. It’s also infinitely more DVR storage than you get with the Genies.

DIRECTV Genie DVRs and wireless Gemini

If you like to keep movies, shows, and sporting events to watch at your leisure, the best option is DIRECTV’s Genie (200 hours, five simultaneous recordings) or wireless Genie 2 (450 hours, three simultaneous recordings). The Genie DVRs don’t auto-delete your recordings unless you’re out of storage.

The basic Genie (wired) has enough storage and tuners for most users. The Genie 2 is best for committed couch creatures and their binge-watching habits, and the unit is wireless so you can avoid cable clutter. Another wireless option is the Gemini (different from the Gemini Air), which includes a universal voice remote, streaming app support, and 450 hours of cloud DVR storage. If you have multiple TVs, you can add DIRECTV on up to seven more screens in your home with additional Genie or Gemini devices ($10.00 a month each). To learn more about DIRECTV whole-home DVR, check out our DIRECTV Genie review.

DIRECTV satellite vs. streaming installation = simple vs. complicated

Installing and setting up DIRECTV’s streaming plans is a breeze. In fewer than five minutes, I downloaded the app to my Roku Ultra and smartphone, signed in, and started browsing what to watch while also building my watchlist. (Note: We haven’t tested the Gemini Air device. When we do, we’ll update this article. For now, it’s safe to assume the setup process will be just as easy.) The installation process is much more complicated, and potentially frustrating, with DIRECTV’s satellite service. It required setting up an install appointment where a technician installed the dish on my roof, drilled cable holes into my house, and set up my Genie DVR. That involved taking time off work and took significantly longer to complete. Also, apartment dwellers may need to have their landlord sign an installation approval form. On the bright side, installation is free of charge.

DIRECTV streaming vs. satellite: Two services, one streaming app

Comparing DIRECTV’s apps is a little tricky. Allow us to elaborate.

DIRECTV streaming app

All streaming services are based around an app that you use on a compatible device like a Chromecast, gaming console, personal computer, smart TV, smartphone, streaming stick, or tablet. So, while the DIRECTV app is an extra way to enjoy your DIRECTV satellite package on the go, it’s the main way to use the streaming service. When I put the DIRECTV app to the test, I quickly understood why it boasts such high ratings—4.6 out of 5.0 on both Google Play and the Apple App Store. From my perspective, the app consistently delivered an easy, intuitive user experience across every platform I tried. One of the first things that struck me was the app’s incredible device compatibility. I found it worked seamlessly with my Android and iOS devices, Amazon Fire TV sticks, Apple TV, Chromecast, NVIDIA Shield, and even my Roku. I also experimented with it on my Google and Roku smart TVs, as well as various internet browsers, and had no issues whatsoever. It’s safe to say you probably already own something that works with it. Now, on the downside, I was a bit disappointed to discover there’s no gaming console support. As a gamer, that’s a feature I’d love to see in the future. But I was pleasantly surprised to find that the app is compatible with the Apple Vision Pro virtual reality device and even select Peloton models, which is a nice touch. The DIRECTV device compatibility page offers all the nitty-gritty details like specific model numbers and operating system requirements.

DIRECTV streaming vs. DIRECTV satellite FAQ

Why you should trust us

At CableTV.com, we constantly analyze prices, plans, and specifications of TV providers like DIRECTV so our readers can make informed buying decisions. We’ve also tested DIRECTV streaming and DIRECTV satellite from signup through activation, and surveyed other customers to see what they thought about providers. To learn more about our process, see our How We Rank page.

Related articles

    • DIRECTV Streaming Review
    • DIRECTV Satellite Review
    • Best TV Providers
Disclaimer

Satellite: ENTERTAINMENT: ($99.99/mo. w/req’d $10/mo. TV Access Fee) w/24-mo. agmt. Autopay and paperless bill req’d. One-time $49.95 activation fee is extra and applies. CHOICE: ($124.98/mo. w/req’d $10/mo. TV Access Fee & Regional Sports Fee of up to $19.99/mo.) w/ 24-mo.agmt. Autopay and paperless bill req’d. One-time $49.95 activation fee is extra and applies. ULTIMATE: ($154.98/mo. w/req’d $10/mo. TV Access Fee & Regional Sports Fee of up to $19.99/mo.) w/ 24-mo.agmt. Autopay and paperless bill req’d. One-time $49.95 activation fee is extra and applies. PREMIER™: ($199.98/mo. w/req’d $10/mo. TV Access Fee & Regional Sports Fee of up to $19.99/mo.) w/ 24-mo. agmt. Autopay and paperless bill req’d. One-time $49.95 activation fee is extra and applies.

Streaming: ENTERTAINMENT: ($99.99/mo. w/$10/mo. Gemini lease fee) Cancel anytime. CHOICE: ($124.98/mo. w/ req’d Regional Sports Fee of up to $19.99/mo. & $10/mo. Gemini lease fee). Cancel anytime. ULTIMATE: ($154.98/mo. w/ req’d Regional Sports Fee of up to $19.99/mo. & $10/mo. Gemini lease fee). Cancel anytime. PREMIER™: ($199.98/mo. w/ req’d Regional Sports Fee of up to $19.99/mo. & Gemini $10/mo. lease). Cancel anytime. OPTIMO MÁS: ($94.99/mo. w/ $10/mo. Gemini lease fee). Cancel anytime.

item.title: Here Comes ‘The Bride!’—Watch Her On VOD
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: “The Bride!” is officially here as of April 7. See where to rent or buy Maggie Gyllenhaal’s latest, how to save $1 on your rental, and when it hits HBO Max!
item.content:encoded:
The CableTV.com take

  • As of today, Maggie Gyllenhaal’s “The Bride!” is streaming at video-on-demand (VOD) outlets.
  • “The Bride!” isn’t on a subscription streaming service yet, but it’ll eventually be on HBO Max.
  • “The Bride!” comes to 4K UHD, Blu-ray, and DVD on Tuesday, May 19, 2026.

Save the date! On Tuesday, April 7, aka today, Maggie Gyllenhaal’s “The Bride!” becomes available to rent or buy at video-on-demand (VOD) outlets. You can find “The Bride!” at digital VOD storefronts like Amazon Prime Video, Apple TV, Fandango at Home, Google TV, Plex, and YouTube Movies & TV. Rentals are generally $19.99, or you can purchase the film for an extra five bucks ($24.99). See below for a list of VOD retailers carrying the film and any price differences (psssst!—there is one).In the film, Christian Bale’s Frank and Jessie Buckley’s Ida—who eventually becomes the titular undead bride—are forced into an arranged marriage with catastrophic results. The catastrophe in question? The Bride develops a mind of her own and begins smashing the patriarchy. (Oh, the horror. Go, grrrl.) The unhappy couple is registered at Macy’s.Pro tip: You can save a dollar by renting “The Bride!” from Plex for only $18.99.

Where to watch “The Bride!”

VOD Digital Storefront Rental price Purchase price
Amazon Prime Video $19.99 $24.99
Apple TV $19.99 $24.99
Fandango at Home $19.99 $24.99
Google TV $19.99 $24.99
Movies Anywhere* Participating Participating
Best price
Plex 		$18.99 —
YouTube Movies & TV $19.99 $24.99

Data as of post date. * Doesn’t rent or sell movies, but allows you to share them.

When will “The Bride!” be on HBO Max?

So when will “The Bride!” be available on subscription-based streaming services? I’ve yet to see a streaming release date for the film, but I can make an educated guess as to where it will land. Since “The Bride!” is a Warner Bros. film, it should stream on HBO Max. Online buzz suggests this could occur in mid-to-late May.

More horror content from CableTV.com

  • Shudder Review
  • Best Streaming Services for Horror Fans
  • How to Watch the Predator Movies in Order

item.title: 7 Best Movies Coming to Netflix in 2026
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: We researched every new Netflix movie streaming in 2026, and curated seven for you. See our expert staff picks.
item.content:encoded:
This week, we’re revisiting our viral TikTok video and our follow-up comprehensive list of upcoming movies on Netflix. Whether you’re into suspenseful crime thrillers or raunchy office comedies, we present to you an even more curated, condensed list of new Netflix movies you need to have on your radar.

7. “People We Meet on Vacation” | Jan. 9 (streaming now)

Right now, everyone is watching “People We Meet on Vacation,” and for good reason. It’s based on the massive Emily Henry novel and stars Tom Blyth and Emily Bader, who have so much chemistry it’s almost unfair. I was obsessed with the book’s friends-to-lovers tension, and seeing Poppy and Alex’s annual summer trips come to life is exactly the sunshine we need in January.

6. “Apex” | April 24

Charlize Theron returns to her action-star roots in “Apex,” where she plays a grieving widow living in the wilderness who is suddenly hunted by a serial killer. I love a good cat-and-mouse thriller, and with Taron Egerton co-starring, the tension in this one is going to be through the roof.

5. “Remarkably Bright Creatures” | May 8

Based on the viral novel, this mystery drama stars Sally Field as Tova, a widow who befriends a giant Pacific octopus at an aquarium. It sounds quirky, but the book was a total tearjerker. I think Tova’s journey toward uncovering the truth about her son’s disappearance will be one of the most moving performances of the year.

4. “The Chronicles of Narnia: The Magician’s Nephew” | Dec. 25

My most anticipated movie of the year is “The Chronicles of Narnia: The Magician’s Nephew,” which is coming next Christmas. Directed by Greta Gerwig (“Barbie” and “Lady Bird”), this is the start of a whole new Narnia era for Netflix. I’ve heard it’s going to have a limited IMAX run in November before it hits the streamer, and I personally can’t wait to see how Gerwig handles the origin of the Wardrobe and the White Witch. (Note: The film gets an IMAX release on Nov. 26.)

3. “Office Romance” TBD 2026

For the rom-com lovers out there, Jennifer Lopez and Brett Goldstein star in the blandly titled “Office Romance.” The name may be snoozy, but I’m particularly excited for this one because Goldstein (our favorite “Ted Lasso” grump) actually co-wrote the script. It’s been teased as a bit more raunchy than J.Lo’s usual fare, which should make for a hilarious and steamy workplace dynamic.

2. “The Whisper Man” | TBD 2026

Robert De Niro in a crime thriller? Count me in. This adaptation of Alex North’s bestseller follows a widowed writer whose son vanishes in a town with a dark history of child abductions. I think adding Michael Keaton to the cast really elevates the grit factor for this one.

1. “Here Comes the Flood” | TBD 2026

Denzel Washington and Robert Pattinson together in a heist movie is all I need to know to get it on my list. That said, the alluring plot, directed by Fernando Meirelles, features a deadly series of double-crosses between a bank security guard and a master thief. I’m already trying to guess the ending, and I know it’s going to keep me on the edge of my seat.

Do our picks make you wanna renew Netflix?

Read our thorough Netflix review, and find out about all of its price hikes and new quirks before you sign up.

item.title: Looking for Your Next Reality Fix? Here Are 7 New Reality Series Streaming in April!
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Our reality TV expert breaks down 7 new reality series coming to streaming in April 2026. Check out their curated picks.
item.content:encoded:
New reality series coming in April 2026

April showers bring reality TV flowers, and we’ve been in a reality TV glow lately. Bravo’s “Summer House” is dominating on Tuesdays at 8 p.m. ET, and “Southern Hospitality” is serving up its finest dish on Bravo on Wednesdays at 9 p.m. ET. But the garden keeps growing because we have more messy drama, tangled friendships, and fragile relationships (for better or worse) sprouting on our television! From Bravo’s now-classic “The Valley” making its Season 3 return to the next messy season of couples testing their loyalty on Netflix’s “Temptation Island,” this April has something for everyone and will be sure to make our jaws drop. So, without further ado, here’s everything you need to know to get your reality TV fix this April.

7. “Love on the Spectrum” (April 1, Netflix)

Netflix’s Emmy-Award winning series comes back for its fourth season on April 1. “Love on the Spectrum” features adults on the autism spectrum looking to dive into the unknown world of dating and love. You can catch all seven episodes of “Love on the Spectrum” out now on Netflix.

6. “The Valley” (April 1, Bravo and Peacock)

Back for its third season on Bravo, the Vanderpump Rules spin-off brings some familiar faces over from “Vanderpump Rules” (Schwartzy is back) but continues to deliver the uncertainty of adulting, while giving us some new faces and drama. You can catch “The Valley” weekly on Wednesdays at 8 p.m. ET on Bravo and watch the next day on Peacock.

5. “The Real Housewives of Rhode Island” (April 2, Bravo and Peacock)

It’s been so long since we’ve gotten a new city in the Real Housewives rotation, and it feels like “The Real Housewives of Rhode Island” (or “RHORI”) is the answer to all of our table-flipping prayers! Catch “The Real Housewives of Rhode Island” premiering on April 2nd at 9 p.m. ET, but moving to Sunday nights at 9 p.m. ET on Bravo. Of course, you can watch the episodes the next day on Peacock.

4. “The Real Housewives of Atlanta” (April 5, Bravo and Peacock)

The Peaches are back! With two new faces and plenty of drama to bring, we can’t wait until the ladies of Atlanta are on our screens again. This Real Housewives show has been on the air now for seventeen seasons, and it doesn’t feel like it’s slowing down one bit. Be sure to watch the season premiere of “The Real Housewives of Atlanta” (“RHOA”) on Sunday at 8 p.m. ET on Bravo, and watch it the next day on Peacock!

3. “The Reunion: Laguna Beach” (April 10, The Roku Channel)

Ready to let the nostalgia wash over you like a tidal wave? The cast of “Laguna Beach” is going to talk about all of the show’s moments, memories, dramas, and behind-the-scenes action that fans only speculated about. This marks the twentieth anniversary of the show (do you feel your bones aching?), so for the lovers of this show, this will be a night to remember. The “Reunion: Laguna Beach” premieres April 10 on the Roku Channel.

2. “Temptation Island” (April 10, Netflix)

As a long-time watcher of this show, it never fails to make me yell at the TV, and this season will absolutely make me (and perhaps you) do the same! “Temptation Island,” hosted by Mark L. Walberg, brings couples to test their relationship and see which ones can survive temptation. Is it messy? Yes. Will you sit and watch every single minute of it? Absolutely. Will you regret it in the morning? We shall see. Netflix will drop all nine episodes on April 10.

1. “Love Island: Beyond the Villa” (April 15, Peacock)

What happens when you follow your favorite (or least favorite) “Love Island” contestants and catch up on their lives far away from the villa in Fiji? You get “Love Island: Beyond the Villa.” For its second season, we’re catching up with Islanders from Season 7 of the hit love competition show and finding out what they’ve been up to since the cameras cut. Have friendships mended? Have couples broken up? Have love found the islanders dumped? Tune into the season premiere of “Love Island: Beyond the Villa” at 9 p.m. ET on Peacock.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts—we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

What to binge next

  • What To Watch on Netflix
  • New Shows Coming to Netflix
  • What To Watch on Peacock

item.title: Bored? Tired of Blah TV? Here Are 5 New Shows and Movies You Might’ve Missed Last Week.
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: This weekend's CableTV.com staff streaming picks include an upstart prestige drama, a reality TV show for thinking folks, a classic horror franchise, and more. Scroll through some trailers and punchy paragraphs, and find the perfect pick for you. Let's hit the couch! 5 CableTV.com staff picks for [...]
item.content:encoded:
This weekend’s CableTV.com staff streaming picks include an upstart prestige drama, a reality TV show for thinking folks, a classic horror franchise, and more. Scroll through some trailers and punchy paragraphs, and find the perfect pick for you. Let’s hit the couch!

5 CableTV.com staff picks for the weekend of April 3, 2026

1. “Your Friends and Neighbors” | Apple TV | Comedy, drama

Season 2 premiere, Friday, April 3: Jon Hamm returns as Coop, the disgraced ex-hedge fund manager who’s turned to robbing his other rich friends and neighbors in order to maintain the lifestyle he’s become accustomed to. Still fearing exposure, Coop finds his life of thievery spiraling into a dangerous game of blackmail. With the feds circling his affluent enclave, his “nice guy” mask is slipping. One episode premieres tonight, followed by one a week through June 5.

2. “Love on the Spectrum” Season 4 | Netflix | Reality

Season 4 premiere, Wednesday, April 1: Go ahead and enjoy your “Love is Blind,” “The Bachelorette,” and “90-Day Fiancé”—I’ll take “Love on the Spectrum” over all of them. Where so many people get all swoony over the other romance reality shows, I get swept up in the authenticity of watching genuinely likeable, radically honest people seek true love. “Love on the Spectrum” isn’t about money and fame, it’s about exceptional people striving to connect authentically with others while navigating a world not built for them. There is beauty in that.

3. “One Piece” | Crunchyroll | Drama, adventure

Season 22 premiere, Sunday, April 5: If you enjoy animation, but maybe not anime, you still should check out the long-running pirate series “One Piece.” Take it from a former non-fan who, thanks to a friend, is now a very selective anime fan: With the new Elbaph arc, OP is now 22 seasons and around 1,200 episodes deep, and ripe for the bingeing. Some tips for anime noobs giving “One Piece” a shot: If you don’t like the obnoxiously loud, Americanized, dubbed anime shows, opt for the subtitled versions. With the original Japanese-language track, the show has a more serious adult feel—but remains wacky and fun. But no matter how you watch “One Piece,” it’s one entertaining trip. Note: Don’t expect dubs for the 26 new episodes, yet—but you can’t binge that fast, can you, noob?

4. “Scream 7” (2026) | VOD | Horror

Okay, so word on the street (read: internet) is that the lucky number seven doesn’t apply to Ghostface’s seventh slash-fest. What I’m tryin’ to say is people say it sucks. Hard. But you didn’t see the other six Scream movies just to nope out on the seventh one, did ya? Now you can save some money by seeing it at home, just in case it really falls on its ghost face. You can rent “Scream 7″ from video-on-demand (VOD) outlets like Prime Video, Google Movies & TV, and Fandango at Home (formerly VUDU) as of Tuesday, March 31.

5. “XO, Kitty” | Netflix | Comedy, drama

Season 3 premiere, Thursday, April 2: This Korean rom-com series (a spinoff of the Netflix original “To All the Boys” film series) returns for a third season. This year, Kitty Song Covey (Anna Cathcart) is a senior at the Korean Independent School of Seoul (KISS—awwww) and (high school) bucket list and her eyes on Min Ho. Expect K-drama hijinks, a long-awaited reunion with big sister Lara Jean (Lana Condor), and fresh faces stirring the Seoul social scene.

Looking for more new shows and movies this weekend? Check out our expert guide that we update weekly or follow us on SmartNews.

item.title: Prime Anime Is Back! Check Out Curated Shows Being Simulcast This Spring.
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: It's time once again for our quarterly anime simulcast round-up. (And no, this isn't an April Fool's joke.) This spring's curated list features an all-time banger, a highly anticipated manga adaptation, a surreal dark fantasy, and more. 10 CableTV.com anime picks for spring 2026 10. [...]
item.content:encoded:
It’s time once again for our quarterly anime simulcast round-up. (And no, this isn’t an April Fool’s joke.) This spring’s curated list features an all-time banger, a highly anticipated manga adaptation, a surreal dark fantasy, and more.

10 CableTV.com anime picks for spring 2026

10. “ONE PIECE” (Elbaph Arc)

Did you come away from the new season of the live-action “One Piece” adaptation thinking, “Man, I wish we could see the land where these giants are from?” Me too. Luckily for us, the anime is wayyy ahead of the live-action, so you can jump ahead and watch the Elbaph Arc this April. Or you could watch all 1,155 episodes leading up to it first, up to you.

9. “Witch Hat Atelier”

”Witch Hat Atelier” is a beloved fantasy manga, and it’s finally getting an anime adaptation ten years after its first publication. Follow Coco, a novice witch who learns a secret technique for performing magic, as she embarks on an adventure with her new mentor, Qifrey.

8. “MARRIAGETOXIN”

Do you think love can bloom, even on a battlefield? Professional assassin Hikaru Gero thinks his profession prohibits him from ever finding love and getting married. But his clan insists he find a wife, so he recruits a beautiful cross-dresser to help him find a match.

7. “Go For It, Nakamura-kun!!”

”Go For It, Nakamura-kun!!” is a popular Boys’ Love manga whose cover became an art meme a few years back. It’s becoming an anime this month, so you’ll get to watch this shy teenager grapple with a crush in full color and with audio for the first time.

6. “Why Sing, When You Can Warble?”

Arata Kouda is a middle schooler whose life changes when he meets Kujima—a weird, anthropomorphic bird-person-thing. “Why Sing, When You Can Warble” is a fun comedy with a unique approach to character design.

5. “That Time I Got Reincarnated as a Slime” Season 4

”That Time I Got Reincarnated as a Slime” is one of the most well-known isekai anime, and it’s come a long way since its first season. After dying and getting reincarnated as a JRPG slime monster with Kirby-like abilities, Satoru Mikami made the most of his new life—and even has a human form now. Which I feel like kind of defeats the purpose of “reincarnated as a slime,” but hey, what do I know?

4. “NEEDY GIRL OVERDOSE”

”NEEDY GIRL OVERDOSE” is the anime spin-off of the video game “NEEDY STREAMER OVERLOAD”—but sometimes the names are switched, just to make life a little harder for your friendly neighborhood anime reporter. While the game is a psychological horror management sim with a ton of different endings, the anime will only focus on one timeline in the life of OMGkawaiiAngel, a streamer and idol with dreams of ultimate popularity.

3. “The Most Heretical Last Boss Queen: From Villainess to Savior” Season 2

”Reincarnated as the villainess in an otome game, now I have to prove I’m a good person so I don’t die” has become such a popular isekai trope in the past couple of years that it might as well be its own genre at this point. And yet I will still watch every single one. This is one of the few that’s not available on Crunchyroll, but the first season is also available on Hulu if you’re not into niche anime streamers like HIDIVE.

2. “Dorohedoro” Season 2

Don’t you hate it when a sorcerer erases your memories and turns you into a big lizard? “Dorohedoro” is a bizarre dark fantasy about Caiman, an amnesiac lizard man seeking answers from the otherworldly beings who treated him like a science experiment. You can find it both on Netflix and Crunchyroll.

1. “Rooster Fighter”

This one got picked up by Adult Swim, so it’s gonna be a little weird. Keiji fights giant monsters in Japan, traveling from city to city while adhering to a strict code of conduct. He’s also out to avenge the death of his sister, Elizabeth. I think that’s about all you need to know … Oh, right. He’s also a rooster.

Looking for more new anime this spring? Check out our comprehensive simulcast guide that we update weekly, or follow us on SmartNews.

item.title: Italian Court Rules Netflix Must Refund Customers. Will It Happen in the US Next?
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: An Italian court ruling found that Netflix must reimburse Italian subscribers after years of price hikes.
item.content:encoded:
A solution for Netflix bill increases?

Streaming service price increases are the one thing everyone dislikes equally, but some subscribers may be getting some overdue relief. Last week, an Italian court ruled that Netflix’s price hikes in the country were unlawful and ordered the streamer to refund eligible Italian subscribers up to €500 euros. According to a court ruling (via Reuters), Netflix’s price hikes violated the country’s Consumer Code—which provides consumer protections for Italian customers—because the streamer didn’t provide a valid reason for them in its subscriber contract. Court officials cited Netflix’s string of Premium plan price increases between 2017 and 2024, which hiked its cost to almost double that of Netflix’s Standard plan. Subscribers are expected to have their Netflix price reduced and receive partial refunds. Netflix says it plans to appeal the Italian court’s decision.

What Netflix price increases mean for you

Streaming prices have gone up and up, and Netflix is no exception. The streamer’s Premium plan is the priciest on the market, and it just dropped another price hike late last month. And to put it mildly, U.S. courts are less likely than their European counterparts to issue similar consumer-friendly rulings. For starters, federal regulators have rubber-stamped numerous mergers that will likely lead to higher prices for customers. They’ve been similarly dismissive of suits against the major streamers. In 2025, a U.S. District Court dismissed a class action lawsuit against Amazon Prime Video over its ad-free tier, arguing that Amazon had clearly stated that it could change the terms of its membership benefits at any time. That said, there are ways to work around these price hikes. Numerous cell phone and internet providers have started bundling free or discounted streaming services with their plans, so you can build a bundle of your own. But if you simply want to pay less for Netflix in the States, your best bet is to downgrade to an ad-supported tier or grin and bear it.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we have years of experience covering the biggest tech and streaming news. Stay tuned with us to learn more about the biggest TV and tech stories and see how they affect you. For more about us, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news

  • Crunchyroll Unveils Anime Awards Nominees: Cast Your Vote Before It’s Too Late
  • Classic ‘Sesame Street’ Episodes Find Another Home, and You Can Watch Them for Free
  • Savannah Bananas Returning to Major TV Channels This Summer

item.title: Crunchyroll Unveils Anime Awards Nominees: Cast Your Vote Before It’s Too Late
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Voting is open! See the 2026 Crunchyroll Anime Awards nominees, find out how to cast your vote, and get the live ceremony streaming schedule.
item.content:encoded:
Crunchyroll announces its Anime Awards nominees

The Crunchyroll Anime Awards are right around the corner, so now’s the time to make your voice heard and vote for your favorite titles. Crunchyroll just unveiled its 2026 nominees, including longtime favorites like “DAN DA DAN,” “My Hero Academia,” and “The Apothecary Diaries” as well as popular newcomers “Gachiakuta” and “The Summer Hikaru Died.” Want to get your vote in before the April 15 deadline? Read on to find out how, and then tune back in on May 23 to watch the ceremony.

How to vote for the Crunchyroll Anime Awards

You have two options for voting in the Crunchyroll Anime Awards: Crunchyroll and MyAnimeList. You have to sign in with your Crunchyroll or MAL credentials, and you can vote once per day. You may not have access to all 32 categories depending on which language dubs you’ve watched with your account. Personally, I found the categories easy to decide until I got to the character awards. You’re telling me I have to choose between Enjin, Shisui, and Turbo Granny for Best Supporting Character?! That’s just cruel. Voting closes on April 15, so get your votes in now!

Crunchyroll Anime Awards nominees

The Crunchyroll Anime Awards honor a lot of different types of anime across 32 categories, even anime that doesn’t air on Crunchyroll itself. This year’s nominees include a lot of stellar titles. Here’s a glimpse at some of the anime you can vote for before April 15.

  • “DAN DA DAN”
  • “Gachiakuta”
  • “My Hero Academia”
  • “Takopi’s Original Sin”
  • “The Apothecary Diaries”
  • “The Summer Hikaru Died”
  • “ZENSHU”
  • “DIGIMON BEATBREAK”
  • “Kaiju No. 8”
  • “SPY x FAMILY”
  • “SAKAMOTO DAYS”
  • “RANMA ½”
  • “My Dress-Up Darling”

You can check Crunchyroll’s website for the full list of nominees in all 32 categories.

How to watch the Crunchyroll Anime Awards

This year’s Crunchyroll Anime Awards will air on Saturday, May 23 at 5:00 p.m. JST, which is 4:00 a.m. EDT for us Americans. So while most of us won’t be able to catch the ceremony live, it’ll still be available on demand on Crunchyroll later in the day.

Who we are

At CableTV.com, we’re not just entertainment experts—we’re also fans just like you. So while our first priority is reporting the best and most accurate information about TV providers and streaming services, we also love talking about our favorite shows and movies. So you can count on us to share our honest opinions and unbridled enthusiasm. For more on our process, check out our Editorial Policy.

More industry news for TV fans

  • Crunchyroll Spring 2026 Lineup
  • Spring 2026 Anime Simulcast Schedule
  • ‘Witch Hat Atelier’ Gets An Anime Adaptation

item.title: The 9 Spiciest ‘Euphoria’ Episodes: From Hazing Violence to That Infamous Bathroom Scene
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Euphoria Season 3 is almost here! We’ve researched the 9 spiciest Euphoria episodes so you don’t have to. Relive every shocking HBO moment before April 12.
item.content:encoded:
You can call “Euphoria” brilliant or contrived, but you definitely can’t call it chill. The HBO series, about a group of teenaged friends in a fictional California suburb, is jam-packed with violent boyfriends, creepy-as-hell drug dealers, wanton sexual activity, and some seriously agonizing depictions of drug addiction. Created by Sam Levinson, “Euphoria” also has great music, smart writing, and a killer cast that includes era-defining stars Zendaya and Sydney Sweeney. But let’s be real, what really gets people talking are the show’s spiciest and most shocking moments, of which there are many. With Season 3 premiering on April 12, here’s a guide to the HBO series’ most extra, outrageous, over-the-top, unsettling episodes.

“Pilot” (Season 1, Episode 1)

”Euphoria” jumps right into the shock and awe as the very first episode features not one, but two sex scenes, both involving adults hooking up with minors. Certainly the most outrageous of the two is when freewheeling transgender teen Jules Vaughn goes to a motel to hook up with Cal Jacobs, a full-grown man and father of Jules’ evil classmate Nate. Going by the ultra-cringe handle DominantDaddy on the dating app where they meet, Cal lays the groundwork for a major plotline when he films their tryst without her consent.

“Stuntin’ Like My Daddy” (Season 1, Episode 2)

After Maddy gets down with an older boy named Tyler in a swimming pool during a party in the pilot, Maddy’s ex-boyfriend Nate hunts down Tyler at his apartment, brutally interrogates him, and beats him senseless. The violence itself is shocking enough, but it’s extra creepy when Nate calmly cleans himself up afterwards and looks at himself in the mirror while Tyler silently whimpers on the floor.

“Shook Ones Pt. II” (Season 1, Episode 4)

Varsity quarterback Nate is by far the scariest and most abusive student at East Highland High School, and if you aren’t already convinced of his dark side, then just consider what he does in “Shook Ones Pt. II.” As one of the plotlines of this acclaimed episode, which revolves around a trip to a carnival, Nate catfishes Jules and coerces her into staying quiet about her relationship with Cal by threatening to report nude photos of her and get her prosecuted for “child pornography.”

“The Next Episode” (Season 1, Episode 6)

College-aged football star Chris McKay is one of the kindest characters on the entire show, but he suffers serious psychological damage in “The Next Episode” when he gets assaulted by a gang of masked frat brothers in front of his girlfriend at a party. The disturbing hazing scene generated a lot of discussion online because the editing makes the attack look like a rape, although Algee Smith (who plays McKay) said later that it was “fake humping.”

“And Salt the Earth Behind You” (Season 1, Episode 8)

A heartbreaking rift between Rue Bennett (played by Zendaya) and her beloved Jules leads to a whimsically haunting relapse when Rue goes home and snorts a line of narcotic powder. Over the off-kilter R&B of Labrinth and Zendaya’s R&B jam “All for Us,” Zendaya enacts a kind of funeral procession as she stumbles outside into a street crowded with dancers and gets held aloft in a reflection of her journey through grief and addiction.

“Trying to Get to Heaven Before They Close the Door” (Season 2, Episode 1)

The unforgettable “I have to pee!” scene goes down when Maddy pounds on the door of the bathroom at yet another depraved dance party full of underage kids. Inside the bathroom that Maddy is trying to get into, her best friend Cassie Howard (played by Sydney Sweeney) is freaking out because she’s right in the middle of getting it on with Maddy’s ex, the infamous Nate. This episode also marks the harrowing introduction of Laurie, a dead-eyed schoolteacher turned drug dealer.

“Ruminations: Big and Little Bullys” (Season 2, Episode 3)

It’s more touching than spicy, but “Ruminations: Big and Little Bullys” is worth a mention for showing Cal’s origin story as a teenage wrestling champion who discovers his sexuality with his best friend Derek when they share a dance and a kiss at a local gay bar. This being “Euphoria” and all, Cal’s dreams of ever having a functional relationship are then crushed when he finds out that his girlfriend (and future wife) Martha is pregnant.

“Stand Still Like a Hummingbird” (Season 2, Episode 5)

In what’s widely regarded as the most painful moment in “Euphoria” history, Rue delivers a monumentally hurtful drug-addict rant to her sister, her mother, and Jules after a suitcase full of drugs that she got from Laurie goes missing. It’s truly one of the most powerful depictions of dope addiction ever captured on TV: Zendaya is all desperate agony and narcissistic shame as she unleashes her barrage of scathing insults.

“The Theater and Its Double” (Season 2, Episode 7)

Although the penultimate episode of Season 2 doesn’t have the same shock value as the bloody finale, it’s worth a shoutout because this is the part when Lexi Howard gets her revenge on all of her obnoxious and self-absorbed East Highland High friends. In a brilliant twist of “Hamlet”-esque meta-narrative, she puts on a school play where all the secret dramas and toxic conflicts of the school are revealed to everyone.

Related articles

  • Shows Like ‘Euphoria‘
  • 12 Margot Robbie Movies and Shows
  • 9 Jacob Elordi Movies and Shows

item.title: 9 Best Jacob Elordi Movies and TV Shows: Ranking His Most Iconic Roles
item.dc:creator: CableTV.com
item.description: Jacob Elordi has moved beyond heartthrob status. From “Saltburn” to his 2026 “Wuthering Heights” release, here are his 9 best movies and shows ranked.
item.content:encoded:
✦ Edited and fact-checked by Senior Staff Writer Randy Harward, our editorial lead with 25+ years covering TV, film, and music.Jacob Elordi has officially successfully shed his “Netflix boyfriend” image to become one of the most sought-after dramatic actors of his generation. We’ve watched him dominate the awards circuit recently for his transformative performance in Guillermo del Toro’s “Frankenstein,” defining a new era of dark, atmospheric dramas. Whether he’s playing a toxic high school athlete or a classic literary anti-hero, Elordi is currently the king of Prestige Mess—choosing projects that are guaranteed to be polarizing, visually stunning, and social-media-friendly.

1. “Frankenstein” (2025)

In a career-defining physical performance, Elordi starred as The Creature in Guillermo del Toro’s gothic masterpiece. The role required extensive prosthetics and saw Elordi winning Best Supporting Actor at the 2026 Critics’ Choice Awards. It’s a haunting, sympathetic portrayal that positions him as a serious heavyweight in the industry, and it’s hard to believe that the role originally went to Andrew Garfield, who dropped out due to scheduling conflicts.

Where to watch “Frankenstein”

At the time of writing, you can watch “Frankenstein” on Netflix, or rent it from video-on-demand (VOD) outlets like Prime Video or Apple TV.

2. “Euphoria” (2019–2026)

After years of production delays, we are finally seeing the return of Nate Jacobs in “Euphoria” Season 3 this April. The new season features a four-year time jump, moving the characters out of high school and into a messy, complicated adulthood. Elordi has hinted that Nate’s storyline this time around is “incredibly liberating” and “singular,” suggesting he may be more isolated from the main ensemble than ever before.

Where to watch “Euphoria”

At the time of writing, you can watch “Euphoria” on HBO Max, or rent it from video-on-demand (VOD) outlets like Prime Video or Apple TV.

3. “Wuthering Heights” (2026)

Set for a wide Valentine’s Day weekend release, this Emerald Fennell-directed adaptation is anything but a safe period piece. Starring alongside Margot Robbie as Heathcliff and Cathy, the film adopts a psychedelic, erotic, and highly stylized tone. While some book purists have debated the casting, the online elite are already praising the film’s prestige-pulp energy.

Where to watch “Wuthering Heights”

At the time of writing, “Wuthering Heights” is available to rent from video-on-demand (VOD) outlets like Prime Video or Apple TV.

4. “Priscilla” (2023)

Elordi took on the monumental task of playing Elvis Presley in Sofia Coppola’s intimate biopic, focusing on the perspective of Priscilla Presley. Unlike other versions of the King, Elordi’s performance highlights the darker, more controlling aspects of the icon behind closed doors. The actor’s towering height and low register brought a unique, brooding presence to the role that captivated critics.

Where to watch “Priscilla”

At the time of writing, you can watch “Priscilla” on Netflix, or rent it from video-on-demand (VOD) outlets like Prime Video or Apple TV.

5. “Saltburn” (2023)

In this polarizing rich-kids-behaving-badly thriller, Elordi plays Felix Catton, the charismatic and wealthy student who becomes the object of obsession. The film cemented his status as a muse for high-concept directors like Emerald Fennell. It’s a visually stunning performance that relies heavily on Elordi’s natural magnetism and effortless cool.

Where to watch “Saltburn”

At the time of writing, you can rent “Saltburn” from video-on-demand (VOD) outlets like Prime Video or Apple TV.

6. “The Kissing Booth” (2018)

The film that started it all. While Elordi has since moved on to much darker fare, we can’t ignore the cultural impact of Noah Flynn. This Netflix original made him an overnight global heartthrob and the quintessential “Netflix boyfriend.” It serves as a fascinating time capsule of his early career before he pivoted to the unsettling roles he prefers today.

Where to watch “The Kissing Booth”

At the time of writing, you can watch “The Kissing Booth” on Netflix, or rent it from video-on-demand (VOD) outlets like Prime Video or Apple TV.

7. “The Sweet East” (2023)

Elordi showed off his indie sensibilities in this picaresque journey through the fringes of American society. Playing Ian, a high-strung actor, he proved he could handle satirical, offbeat dialogue just as well as intense drama. It’s a small but memorable role that signaled his desire to work with experimental filmmakers.

Where to watch “The Sweet East”

At the time of writing, you can watch “The Sweet East” on Hulu, or rent it from video-on-demand (VOD) outlets like Prime Video or Apple TV.

8. “Deep Water” (2022)

In this psychological thriller starring Ben Affleck and Ana de Armas, Elordi plays a young piano teacher who gets caught in the middle of a deadly marital game. While his screen time is limited, his character’s fate is a pivotal turning point in the film’s dark narrative. It was one of the first signs that Elordi was interested in the prestige pulp genre.

Where to watch “Deep Water”

At the time of writing, you can watch “Deep Water” on Hulu, or rent it from video-on-demand (VOD) outlets like Prime Video or Apple TV.

9. “On Swift Horses” (2024)

In this 1950s period drama, Elordi plays Julius, a Korean War veteran living a secret life. The film explores themes of luck, love, and the American dream through a mid-century lens. It’s a quiet, internal performance that showcases his ability to convey deep emotion with very few words.

Where to watch “On Swift Horses”

At the time of writing, you can watch “On Swift Horses” on Netflix, or rent it from video-on-demand (VOD) outlets like Prime Video or Apple TV.

Frequently asked questions

Is Jacob Elordi in the new "Frankenstein" movie?

Yes! Jacob Elordi stars as The Creature in Guillermo del Toro's "Frankenstein," which was released in late 2025. He replaced Andrew Garfield in the role and has already won several major awards for his performance.

When does "Euphoria" Season 3 come out?

After several delays, "Euphoria" Season 3 is officially confirmed to premiere on HBO and Max on April 12, 2026. The season will feature a significant time jump, following the characters into their early twenties.

Who does Jacob Elordi play in "Wuthering Heights?"

Jacob Elordi plays the iconic role of Heathcliff in the 2026 adaptation of "Wuthering Heights," directed by Emerald Fennell. He stars alongside Margot Robbie, who plays Catherine "Cathy" Earnshaw.

What to read next

  • Best Shows Like ‘Euphoria‘
  • Returning Shows We’re Excited for in 2026
  • What to Watch on HBO Max Right Now
  • HBO’s ‘Euphoria’ Season 3 is Back
CableTV.com editors created this article using original analysis and proprietary inputs, with AI tools assisting in drafting. The final content was reviewed and edited for accuracy and quality.
